assessor_1_raw: This is a well-designed ranking experiment. The temporal split is appropriate for non-stationary search logs. The position-bias control (excluding positions 1-2 from training) is a recognized technique. Label quality is addressed via pairwise construction with purchase-weighted grades. The baseline receives fair tuning opportunity. NDCG@10 is appropriate for ranking evaluation. Secondary metrics including IPW-adjusted NDCG, tail query NDCG, and seller quality lift comprehensively address alternative explanations. The scope is appropriately modest. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The experiment demonstrates strong methodological awareness. Temporal splitting with query-frequency and seller-quality stratification is thorough. The position-1/2 exclusion from training with retention in evaluation is an accepted approach. The purchase-weight hyperparameter is tuned via validation, not hardcoded. The baseline is equipped with equivalent tuning and post-processing. The IPW secondary metric addresses circular evaluation concerns. Confound controls (stratified analysis, seller quality lift, exposure adjustment) are comprehensive. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: This learning-to-rank experiment design is rigorous. The core comparison is fair: both LTR model and BM25 receive seller re-ranking post-processing and equivalent tuning effort. The label construction methodology is principled, with position-1/2 bias explicitly handled. The 18-month timeline with temporal split mirrors deployment. NDCG@10 is the right primary metric for ranking quality. The stratified analysis across query quintiles and seller quality quintiles is excellent. The seller quality lift and IPW metrics are exactly the right checks. I find no genuine methodological flaw. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: All three independent assessors reached the same verdict: defense_wins. Unanimous agreement. The experiment design is methodologically sound with appropriate controls, fair baseline comparison, and comprehensive secondary metrics.
